summary: Create abstract 30-node graph fixture and Content Compiler v0
acceptance_criteria: Content Compiler v0 compiles an abstract M1 graph fixture with 30 nodes and at least 60 edges into a runtime content pack with stable IDs and a manifest hash.
Fixture is explicitly synthetic kernel data, not formal map geometry, historical scenario content, economy, population, vassal politics, diplomacy, war, siege, combat, or history database.
Compiler validates unique IDs, stable ID assignment, references, duplicate edges, graph connectivity or explicitly marked isolation, edge direction semantics, and deterministic output ordering.
Runtime content pack can be consumed by sim-core without runtime filesystem access or display-name-based entity lookup.
Bad fixture tests fail for missing references, duplicate IDs, duplicate routes, unstable order, isolated unmarked nodes, and invalid schema.
No runtime SQL/ORM, server, arbitrary code execution, remote URL execution, or new unapproved production dependency is introduced.
If real historical names, claims, cultural labels, or map facts enter scope, task must stop and route to historical_researcher before implementation continues.
required_tests: Compiler unit tests cover valid 30-node fixture and bad-reference/duplicate/isolated-node failures.
Determinism test proves repeated compiles of the same source produce the same manifest hash and runtime pack ordering.
notes: This is an abstract graph fixture for deterministic kernel testing only. It must not become M2 map/content scope.
Because no real historical claims are in scope, qa_reviewer is the final reviewer; historical_researcher is required only if the scope changes.
